titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Chinese Indentured Laborers in Cuba Collection
Chinese Indentured Laborers in Cuba Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Jana Krentz

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 12, 2020 at 09:25

did.unittitle: Chinese indentured laborers in Cuba collection
did.abstract: Official documents regarding Chinese indentured laborers in Cuba in the nineteenth century. The documents include death certificates, new contracts, official petitions to the court, documents outlining the movement of workers, documents concerning fugitive people and imprisoned laborers, arrest warrants, and identification documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Official documents regarding Chinese indentured laborers, often referred to as "asiático" (Asian), in Cuba in the nineteenth century. The documents include death certificates, new contracts, official petitions to the court, documents outlining the movement of workers, documents concerning fugitive people and imprisoned laborers, arrest warrants, and identification documents.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Latin American Collection Curator, 2020. Purchased from Kevin Jackson, 2019.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: During the nineteenth century, around 125,000 Chinese people, predominantly men, were brought to Cuba to work as indentured laborers under conditions similar to, if not actual, enslavement. They primarily worked as laborers in the sugar industry, shipping, mining, and building railroads, among other industries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Some materials are arranged into groupings and are arranged chronologically within those groupings. Other materials are arranged in no particular order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Chinese Indentured Laborers in Cuba Collection (MS 2117). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folder titles were supplied by Yale University Library processing staff based on document contents. Processing staff included names of people in the folder descriptions when they were identified in the materials.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Scope and contents notes for the death certificates were created by Yale University Library processing staff from translated transcriptions of the first lines of the certificates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Socorro, a "free" Asian person from Macao, age 32, died in the hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Crispin, a "free" Asian person from Macao, died in the early hours of the morning in the hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuel, a "free" Asian man from Canton, single, age 29, died in the hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An unidentified Asian man died at 9:00 pm, while he was in the observation room, of acute menengitis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Timoteo, a Canton native, died in the infirmary of the jail. He was a "confined Asian."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Basilio Fernandez, an Asian man, was incarcerated in the Havana prison. He was a "confined Asian" from Canton, son of "incognitos" (unknowns), and died from cirrhosis "hepatica" in the infirmary of the prison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A petition with a list of ten Chinese names to obtain permission to obtain a "recontrata" for them to work in a plantation (ingenio) after fulfilling their primitive contracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formal petition to have 16 "colonos" given permission for their recontrata.

did.unittitle: Formal petition regarding sixteen Chinese laborers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Petition for 16 Chinese people to obtain permission to obtain recontratas to work in an "ingenio" (plantation).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Petition for fifteen Chinese people to obtain permission to obtain a recontrata after having completed their initial primitive contract working in an "ingenio" (plantation).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript petition from the Central Commission of Colonization discussing ten Asian laborers who have fulfilled their primitive contracts and are petitioning to have their Recontrata signed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript from Central Commission of Colonization discussing the "Recontrata" of sixteen "colonos" in a sugar plantation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes three documents pertaining to the movement of a group of Asian laborers.

did.unittitle: Notification of agreement regarding a plantation owner waiting to receive Asian workers to put under contract

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notification that an agreement has been received from the Central Commission of Colonization regarding the owner of a plantation who is waiting to receive Asian workers to put under contract.

did.unittitle: Letter seeking to locate Asian workers and their documentation for an ingenio
Letter indicating that the Asian workers have moved locations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter directed to the Central Commission of Colonization letting them know that the Asian workers have moved locations.

did.unittitle: Manuscript regarding seventy Asian laborers working on the railroad

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript, eight pages, stating that the Company of the Railroad of Puerto Principe has asked for seventy Asian laborers to work on the railroad.

did.unittitle: Document pertaining to Timoteo Chan, an Asian man, declaring himself to be free

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Timoteo Chan shows himself to be "free," and he wants a papers, according to paperwork from the Portuguese Consult; he is not listed in an registry.

did.unittitle: Manuscript stating that Asian laborers have been approved to work at Ceiba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript states that a group of Asian laborers have been approved to work at Ceiba, and they are to be registered and provided with identification cards (cedulas).

did.unittitle: Manuscripts regarding moving two Asian laborers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two manuscripts regarding moving two Asian laborers for the "Ingenio Asuncion" (plantation) to Matanzas. They were captured and delivered the same day. The second document discusses moving one of the Asian laborers in particular.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document from the Jefatura de Policia requesting a baptismal certificate for an Asian male. His godfather is listed, as well as the man's "Patrono.".

did.unittitle: Document pertaining to a fugitive Chinese man

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses an Asian man who arrived oaboard a ship. He has been a fugitive for four years.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document discussing money paid for two fugitive Asian people working for a sugar plantation who were recaptured.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts addressed to a Marquis discussing two fugitive Asian people
Document pertaining to two imprisoned Asian people

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses two Asian people in the Depository for Cimarrones (rebel slaves).

did.unittitle: Manuscript discussing the delivery of two fugitive Asian people from an "ingenio" (sugar plantation)
Petition to the court that a forty-year-old Asian man named Isidoro has escaped and is being pursued
Petition by owner to recontract laborers
Notification that laborers have completed their contract
Identification cards for Pedro Saler, Venacio Riesgo, and Enrilio Galves, indentured laborers from Canton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes twelve contracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes seven contracts.
